other_observers: Brandon Percival, a.m. - Mark Peterson and Brandon, p.m.; Brandon found the bird at about 7:15 a.m. I arrived at 7:30 and we heard the bird periodically until we eventually saw it again off and on for an hour.
duration: observed for a total of less than 10 minutes over an hour of searching, a.m. and p.m.
specific_location: Adjacent to a storage barn at the banding station at Chico Basin Ranch
description: This was a tiny, actively flitting warbler that was all whitish underneath, or with a little buffy tones on the upper breast. The head was very dull whitish gray with about half the crown collored a dull rust that was visible when the bird bend down to feed which it did regularly. It appeared to have a tiny, beady dark eye that was surrounded by a narrow whitish eyering. The short spiky bill was black. I didn't get great views of the dorsal surface and I never did see a rusty rump patch. This was mostly a warbler without field marks, the best was the crown patch and the eyering and overall small size.Behaviors: Flighty, foraging for insects in the developing seeds clusters on plains cottonwoods, unfortunately foraging mostly just under the canopy so great views were lacking. It would hang upside down a few times searching also emerging leaves for insects.Call: similar to Virginia Warbler, one note, fairly loud, partially ventriliqualPlumage: adult - probably male
photographer: Photographer: bill maynard, bill maynard, bill maynard
